answer:
We are told college graduates and non-graduates are equally represented, so half of all sales staff are college graduates and half are not.  
There are 72 level-1 employees, 20 of whom are college graduates, so level-1 non-graduates are \(72 - 20 = 52\).  
Total sales staff is twice the number of college graduates; college graduates total \(20\) (level-1) plus all level-2 graduates. Since total graduates = total non-graduates, and total staff = \(72 + \text{level-2}\), we solve to find level-2 non-graduates = 32.  

\boxed{D}
